about.p1: Edison High School's Boys Beach Volleyball program is one of the top club programs in Orange County. The team competes in the Orange County Volleyball Beach League (OCVBL), a club sport featuring some of the most competitive schools in Southern California. This is not a CIF program.
about.p2: The program runs as a fall sport, typically beginning the week before school starts and running through playoffs in mid-to-late October. Athletes develop the serving, passing, setting, and competitive instincts needed to go up against Orange County's best and earn their spot at the next level.
about.p3: The team practices at Newland Beach Volleyball Courts in Huntington Beach, right in the heart of Surf City USA, the same sand that has produced some of the country's finest beach volleyball players.

coaches.name: Brian Boone
coaches.bio: An Edison High School alumnus and former collegiate indoor player at UCLA, Coach Boone brings an elite competitive background to the program. He also serves as an Edison US History and CIBACS teacher — deeply rooted in the school and community he coaches for. Under his leadership the Chargers have become one of Orange County's most competitive OCVBL programs.

coaches.name: Nathan Cumme & Luke Wright
coaches.bio: Nathan and Luke bring additional coaching depth to the Chargers, supporting player development at Newland Beach Volleyball Courts throughout the fall season.

news.title: 🏥 Physical Day — May 30, 2026
news.body: All students must have a physical on file to participate in high school summer camps or sports during the school year. Physical Day is May 30, 2026 from 10 AM – 2 PM at the Edison Gym. Cost: $30 cash or check.
